arts.ttl: Axel Tuanzebe SNUBS Wayne Rooney as he names 34-year-old World Cup winner as his best ever Man United team-mate
arts.sum: Axel Tuanzebe emerged through Man United's youth academy before going on to make his debut for the club in 2017 and went on to work with a whole host of superstar players.

arts.ttl: Football Daily | All cuts, no glory: Manchester United’s marginal gains with Ratcliffe
arts.sum: Sign up now! Sign up now! Sign up now? Sign up now! Should you ever read comments below assorted lines or on social media abominations, there’s a growing trend of readers asking to stop hearing about Manchester United. No longer a big club, they say. Rubbish at football, they say, quite reasonably. Many of them may be actual United fans. But the fall of a giant will always be a story. Beyond wars, what story from the 20th century was bigger than the sinking of the Titanic? So on Football Daily rolls, because frankly, United are giving this stuff away. But not free lunches to staff. Certainly not rolls, fruit only. Presumably Mike Glogower (yesterday’s Footballl Daily letters) had the famous French comic album series Asterix the Gaul in mind when declaring Manchester City to be ‘Atrocius’, since he sounds like a likely ally of main antagonist Julius Caesar. There is however a football connection since the story is that, when Caesar returned from a campaign over the Alps and falsely claimed to have killed 20,000 Gauls, Asterix asserted that he had fact-checked the claim and that the true figure was only 10,000. Caesar however countered: ‘You are forgetting, Asterix, that, in Europe, away Gauls count double!’” – Adrian Irving. Tottenham Hotspur want to be known as ‘Tottenham Hotspur’, eh (yesterday’s Football Daily, full email edition)? That’s not going to be hard for long-time readers of this tea-timely email. For example, I’ve been referring to Noble Francis as ‘Noble Francis’ – not ‘Noble’, not ‘Francis’ – from the first time I read a Noble Francis letter from Noble Francis” – Mike Wilner. This is an extract from our daily football email … Football Daily. To get the full version, just visit this page and follow the instructions. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: David Moyes confirms Everton talks with PGMOL over Man Utd penalty controversy
arts.sum: Everton boss David Moyes was left furious after his side had a late penalty chalked off in the weekend draw with Manchester United and he has since been in touch with referee bosses since the call

arts.sum: Christopher Vivell left the Blues in 2023 following just seven months at Stamford Bridge

arts.ttl: Man Utd Transfer News: Red Devils appoint Christopher Vivell as full-time director of recruitment - David Ornstein
arts.sum: In lieu of a sporting director, Man Utd have now made the decision to appoint Christopher Vivell as their full-time director of recruitment, according to David Ornstein of The Athletic . Manchester United have appointed Christopher Vivell as their full-time director of recruitment, according to David Ornstein.

arts.ttl: We must take reponsibility for Man Utd problems - Amorim
arts.sum: Manchester United head coach Ruben Amorim says his players must take some responsibility for the club's present financial predicament.
